evaluation_criteria: Mentions user research, personas, and target audience needs.
Discusses hierarchy, accessibility (WCAG), and responsive layout.
Focuses on usability, user flows, and interactive consistency.
Understands stateless vs stateful widgets and rendering cycles.
Explains state management solutions like Provider, Riverpod, or Bloc.
Discusses asynchronous operations, futures, and streams.
Explains differences between database engines (relational vs non-relational).
Understands MVC design patterns, routing, and database indexes.
Mentions security best practices like authentication and password hashing.
Understands Python-specific syntax constructs like list comprehensions.
Explains decorators, generators, or context managers correctly.
Mentions memory management or performance differences.
Discusses analytics, conversion rates, and performance marketing indicators.
Identifies on-page and off-page marketing tactics.
Displays strong verbal communication, confidence, and structures responses.
Uses the STAR method (Situation, Task, Action, Result) for conflict resolution questions.
Shows alignment with company culture and values.
